stages.title: Site Analysis
stages.narration: Stage 1. Site analysis. Earthbag structures perform best when drainage is carefully planned. Water must always be directed away from the foundation.
stages.explanation: Earthbag structures perform best when drainage is carefully planned. Water must always be directed away from the foundation.

stages.title: Setting Out
stages.narration: Stage 2. Setting out. Survey grid and wall centerlines are established on the building platform.
stages.explanation: Accurate layout ensures proper geometry and structural stability.

stages.title: Excavation
stages.narration: Stage 3. Excavation. Foundation trench is cut to expose competent bearing soil.
stages.explanation: Foundation excavation removes weak surface material and provides a stable base.

stages.title: Rubble Trench Foundation
stages.narration: Stage 4. Rubble trench foundation. Large stones and compacted gravel improve drainage beneath the earthbag walls.
stages.explanation: Rubble trench foundations improve drainage and reduce moisture-related damage.

stages.title: First Earthbag Course
stages.narration: Stage 5. The first earthbag course. Polypropylene bags are filled with soil, compacted, and placed in the foundation trench.
stages.explanation: The first earthbag layer forms the structural base of the wall.

stages.title: Barbed Wire Reinforcement
stages.narration: Stage 6. Two strands of barbed wire are now installed between earthbag courses. These strands act as horizontal reinforcement and prevent sliding between layers during seismic loading.
stages.explanation: Barbed wire prevents sliding between bag layers during earthquake shaking.

stages.title: Earthbag Wall Construction
stages.narration: Stage 7. Earthbag walls rise course by course. Each layer is compacted and reinforced with barbed wire.
stages.explanation: Each compacted layer acts as a structural ring. Together they form a monolithic wall system.

stages.title: Vertical Reinforcement
stages.narration: Stage 8. Vertical reinforcement bars are inserted through multiple bag courses and anchored at the base.
stages.explanation: Vertical reinforcement improves resistance against overturning and seismic forces.

stages.title: Window and Door Frames
stages.narration: Stage 9. Door and window frames are set. Walls continue around openings with additional care at corners.
stages.explanation: Openings are structural weak points and require additional reinforcement.

stages.title: RC Lintel Band
stages.narration: Stage 10. Reinforced concrete lintel band is cast. The band wraps the structure and ties all walls together.
stages.explanation: The lintel band ties all walls together and significantly improves seismic performance.

stages.title: Roof Anchorage System
stages.narration: Stage 11. Roof anchor bolts and tie-down connectors secure the roof to the wall system.
stages.explanation: The roof must be securely connected to prevent separation during earthquakes and wind events.

stages.title: Roof Construction
stages.narration: Stage 12. Lightweight timber roof trusses and sheeting are installed to minimize seismic demand.
stages.explanation: Lightweight roofs reduce seismic loads acting on the structure.

stages.title: Plaster and Weather Protection
stages.narration: Stage 13. Wire mesh, plaster, and waterproof coating protect earthbag walls from moisture.
stages.explanation: Earthbag walls require protection from prolonged moisture exposure.

stages.title: Drainage Apron
stages.narration: Stage 14. Drainage apron and surface grading direct rainwater away from the structure.
stages.explanation: Proper drainage dramatically increases the service life of earthbag structures.

stages.title: Completed Structure
stages.narration: Construction complete. The earthbag masonry structure is a flexible, energy-dissipating system suitable for mountainous and seismic regions.
stages.explanation: Fully assembled earthquake-resistant earthbag house with bands, anchors, and weather protection.

foundation.title: Rubble Trench Foundation
foundation.purpose: Drained bearing platform that keeps earthbag walls dry and stable.
foundation.loadMechanism: Rubble and gravel disperse water; compacted base supports wall loads.

earthbag.title: Earthbag Unit
earthbag.materialComposition: Woven polypropylene bag filled with moist stabilized soil (often 5–10% cement or lime in wet climates).
earthbag.compactionProcess: Manual tamping in 10–15 cm lifts until bag is firm and crease-free.
earthbag.strengthMechanism: High density soil develops passive earth pressure; interlock between courses resists sliding.

barbed_wire.title: Barbed Wire Reinforcement
barbed_wire.interlayerFriction: Barbs embed in bag fabric increasing friction between courses.
barbed_wire.shearResistance: Two strands per course act as horizontal shear keys under seismic loading.
barbed_wire.seismicBehavior: Prevents independent layer sliding during cyclic shaking.

vertical_reinforcement.title: Vertical Reinforcement
vertical_reinforcement.anchorage: Bars lapped into plinth or foundation with minimum development length.
vertical_reinforcement.lateralResistance: Connects courses against overturning at wall ends and openings.

lintel_band.title: RC Lintel Band
lintel_band.wallTying: Continuous ring beam at top of wall ties orthogonal walls.
lintel_band.loadRedistribution: Redistributes seismic inertia to all walls as a box.

roof_anchorage.title: Roof Anchorage
roof_anchorage.upliftResistance: Anchor bolts resist wind and seismic uplift on roof diaphragm.
roof_anchorage.earthquakePerformance: Prevents roof separation which causes progressive collapse.
